In which, Sunday doesn’t yet have a room on the express, so you’ll have to share. But… there’s no extra mattresses. You’ll have to share the bed, too.

INTRO PREVIEW

Then, you turned to face him, and he felt the shift in the air before he dared look. Against his better judgment, his eyes flicked to yours, and the softness there struck him like a physical blow.

All rationale left his mind. His heart thundered in his chest, so loud he was sure you could hear it. He swallowed thickly, trying to remember how to breathe, how to think.

“Sunday,” you said, your voice gentle and questioning, and the sound of his name on your lips unraveled what little composure he had left.

He inhaled sharply, his body tense as though holding himself back from something reckless. “You’re—” He stopped himself, the words catching in his throat. His hand twitched at his side, aching to move but unsure of what he wanted to do.

The space between you felt impossibly small, and blood rushed to his cock. Your gaze didn’t waver, and it was too much. Too soft. Too close. Too everything.
